items.title: The $6 Billion Chinese Startup Trying to Build Hands for Every Robot
items.summary: LinkerBot makes dexterous robotic hands for as little as $600. It wants to become the standard for humanoids and automated factories—and eventually replace human labor altogether.

items.title: Meta Copies Snapchat’s Homework Again With ‘Plus’ Features for Instagram and Facebook
items.summary: Meta’s upcoming Instagram Plus and Facebook Plus subscriptions are the latest example of the company seeing what works elsewhere and mimicking it.

items.title: The Pentagon Knew Enemies Could Track Troops’ Phones for Years. Now They Are
items.summary: The US military has long known that cheap fixes could stop location data from exposing its troops. It adopted almost none—and now says adversaries are using the data to target soldiers during a war.

items.title: The internet is being rebuilt for machines
items.summary: As AI agents move from experiments to production, AWS, Cloudflare, and others are redesigning cloud infrastructure for a future dominated by machine-generated internet traffic instead of human users.

items.title: Slate Auto will announce pricing and take preorders for its EV on June 24
items.summary: The Bezos-backed EV startup has yet to announce final pricing for its vehicle, which is supposed to start shipping by the end of this year.

items.title: Asana acquires no-code agent-builder StackAI
items.summary: Asana will incorporate StackAI into its growing suite of AI workflow tools.

items.title: US and Iran reach tentative deal to extend ceasefire, US officials say
items.summary: But there has been conflicting information from Tehran, with one news agency reporting that no deal has been finalised or confirmed.

items.title: Are US and Iran close to peace or sliding back to war?
items.summary: Neither side seems interested in a return to all-out conflict, despite the latest exchange of strikes.

items.title: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
items.summary: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

items.title: CFTC sues Rhode Island over actions against prediction markets
items.summary: It marks the seventh state the commission has sued in a dispute over who has the right to regulate event contract platforms.

items.title: Gaming association says states have lost $1 billion in tax revenue due to prediction markets
items.summary: Bill Miller, American Gaming Association president and CEO, added that prediction market platforms in his view amount to "backdoor sports betting."

items.title: Nio shares jump 10% after releasing first flagship EV in more than two years
items.summary: Chinese electric car company Nio has launched two lower-priced brands in the last two years to broaden its customer base in a sluggish Chinese consumer market.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Police respond to ‘swatting’ call at Justice Amy Coney Barrett’s home
items.summary: Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett was the victim of a “swatting” call Wednesday night at her Virginia home, according to local police.  Police radio traffic shows law enforcement received a call for “suspicious noise of gunshots” at Barrett’s suburban Virginia address. The dispatcher warned responding officers shortly afterward that a “high priority resident of the county” lived there and the report may not be authentic. When reached for comment, Fairfax County…

items.title: Los Angeles mayoral race draws attention with viral moments, surprise rise of Bass challenger Pratt
items.summary: ATLANTA — The Los Angeles mayoral race has increasingly drawn national attention, transforming from a local contest into one of the more unconventional municipal races in recent memory. Mayor Karen Bass (D) faces a challenge on Tuesday from 15 candidates, including former reality TV star Spencer Pratt, whose candidacy has surged from novelty to serious contender. The nonpartisan primary sends…

items.title: US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya to open Friday
items.summary: Trump administration officials confirmed Thursday that they will not allow any American who was exposed to Ebola back into the country.  A 50-bed field hospital quarantine facility for Americans that is being constructed in Kenya will be operational on Friday, according to senior administration officials.  If someone tests positive or develops symptoms, they will be sent elsewhere for advanced care. Officials weren’t clear where such patients would be sent but said…

items.title: Short Sellers Are Betting Record Amounts Against Stocks. But the Market is Rallying On a Potential Deal Between Trump and Iran.
items.summary: Short Sellers Are Betting Record Amounts Against Stocks. But the Market is Rallying On a Potential Deal Between Trump and Iran.

items.title: Stocks Mixed Awaiting Fresh Iran News
items.summary: Stocks Mixed Awaiting Fresh Iran News

items.title: Micron replaces mighty Walmart in the $1 trillion club
items.summary: Micron replaces mighty Walmart in the $1 trillion club

items.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles’ lost crocodiles
items.summary: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles’ vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens. The reptiles were not a unique species after all, but an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean.

items.title: Arctic Ocean passed a tipping point and scientists say it may never recover
items.summary: The Arctic Ocean may have crossed a dangerous tipping point. Scientists say the rapid disappearance of sea ice is triggering a hidden chemical shift that is stripping the ocean of nitrate — a nutrient essential for the tiny plankton that support Arctic life. As nitrate levels plunge, the entire food web could feel the impact, from fish and seabirds to whales and polar ecosystems.

items.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
items.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds. The research suggests these wrens are evolving independently, developing unique songs, appearances, and genetics that may eventually turn them into entirely new species.

items.title: Homing pigeons may use a surprising navigation mechanism
items.summary: How animals navigate by Earth's magnetic field is hotly debated. New research in pigeons points to iron-laden liver immune cells as the compass.

items.title: Can DEET attract mosquitoes? A lab study offers clues
items.summary: Lab experiments suggest mosquitoes can smell DEET and learn to associate it with food, but it’s unclear whether that happens in the wild.

items.title: A $4 tongue swab test detects tuberculosis within 30 minutes
items.summary: The new test may catch active tuberculosis in those with low access to health care or who have trouble making the phlegm needed for traditional tests.
